##NAMES=Meieranite
##RRUFFID=R140947
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_2_Sr_3_MgSi_6_O_17_
##LOCALITY=Wessels mine, Kalahari manganese field, Northern Cape Province, South Africa
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Jaco and Tania Janse van Nieuwenhuizen
##DESCRIPTION=Aggregates of granular blue crystals, associated with massive pale green sugilite, prismatic grey aegirine and bladed white pectolite. Type sample
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140947
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Na_1.96_(Sr_2.91_Ba_0.03_Ca_0.03_Pb_0.02_)(Mg_0.62_Mn_0.28_Co_0.07_Fe_0.01_)Si_6.03_O_17_ ; = bright phase. Grey phase is sugilite.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.938 b: 10.4923 c: 18.256 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 1520.5 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
